pre_game.message: Hey, I’m E. This is a pretty intense way to spend a day, isn't it? Have you ever done anything like this before?
Hey E, I'm H. It definitely is. This is my first time, but I'm pretty focused on winning today. How about you?
I'm in the same boat—first timer, and very focused. I've got a family of five counting on me, so the stakes feel pretty high today. What's driving you to win?
I’ve got a loan due next month that’s been weighing on me, so I’m here to clear that and take care of the people I love.
I completely understand that pressure; debt is a heavy burden when you're just trying to provide. It sounds like we’re both here for the same reasons. What do you do for work normally?
I work in administration for a local firm; it pays the bills, but it’s definitely not enough to clear a debt like this. What about you?
I was in finance, but I actually got laid off recently. It’s been a rough few weeks trying to find something new while being the only one bringing in a paycheck.

That’s a lot of pressure to carry on your own, especially with a family of five. It really highlights why we're both so determined today. Do you think your finance background will help you with the game strategy?
I hope so. I’m used to analyzing risk and numbers, but it’s a lot harder to stay objective when it’s my own family’s survival on the line.
I hear you. It's much harder when the stakes are personal, but maybe that's the edge we need to stay sharp. Let's see how it goes.
I agree, that drive can make all the difference. Good luck to you, H—I hope things work out for your family and that loan.

post_game.message: Thank you for taking exactly your fair share. I wish more of us had followed your example instead of letting greed take over.
You're welcome. I believe that being fair is more important than maximizing personal gain at the expense of others.
